## еұһжҖ§

### context

```ts
context: unknown;
```

If using the new style context, re-declare this in your class to be the
`React.ContextType` of your `static contextType`.
Should be used with type annotation or static contextType.

#### зӨәдҫӢ

```ts
static contextType = MyContext
// For TS pre-3.7:
context!: React.ContextType<typeof MyContext>
// For TS 3.7 and above:
declare context: React.ContextType<typeof MyContext>
```

#### еҸӮйҳ…

[React Docs](https://react.dev/reference/react/Component#context)

#### е®ҡд№үдәҺ

.pnpm/@types+react@18.3.28/node\_modules/@types/react/ts5.0/index.d.ts:1015

### contextType?

```ts
static optional contextType: Context<any>;
```

If set, `this.context` will be set at runtime to the current value of the given Context.

#### зӨәдҫӢ

```ts
type MyContext = number
const Ctx = React.createContext<MyContext>(0)

class Foo extends React.Component {
  static contextType = Ctx
  context!: React.ContextType<typeof Ctx>
  render () {
    return <>My context's value: {this.context}</>;
  }
}
```

#### еҸӮйҳ…

[https://react.dev/reference/react/Component#static-contexttype](https://react.dev/reference/react/Component#static-contexttype)

#### е®ҡд№үдәҺ

.pnpm/@types+react@18.3.28/node\_modules/@types/react/ts5.0/index.d.ts:997

## ж–№жі•

### componentDidCatch()?

```ts
optional componentDidCatch(error: Error, errorInfo: ErrorInfo): void
```

Catches exceptions generated in descendant components. Unhandled exceptions will cause
the entire component tree to unmount.

#### еҸӮж•°

| иҢғеӣҙ | зұ»еһӢ |
| ------ | ------ |
| `error` | `Error` |
| `errorInfo` | `ErrorInfo` |

`void`

`ComponentLifecycle.componentDidCatch`

### componentDidMount()?

```ts
optional componentDidMount(): void
```

### componentDidUpdate()?

```ts
optional componentDidUpdate(
   prevProps: Readonly<P>, 
   prevState: Readonly<S>, 
   snapshot?: SS): void
```

Called immediately after updating occurs. Not called for the initial render.

The snapshot is only present if [getSnapshotBeforeUpdate](/api/react/Class.Component.mdx#getsnapshotbeforeupdate) is present and returns non-null.

#### еҸӮж•°

#### иҝ”еӣһ

`void`

### ~~componentWillMount()?~~

```ts
optional componentWillMount(): void
```

Called immediately before mounting occurs, and before [Component.render](/api/react/Class.Component.mdx#render).
Avoid introducing any side-effects or subscriptions in this method.

Note: the presence of NewLifecycle.getSnapshotBeforeUpdate getSnapshotBeforeUpdate
or StaticLifecycle.getDerivedStateFromProps getDerivedStateFromProps prevents
this from being invoked.

#### иҝ”еӣһ

`void`

#### е·Іиў«ејғз”Ё

16.3, use ComponentLifecycle.componentDidMount componentDidMount or the constructor instead; will stop working in React 17

#### еҸӮйҳ…

 - [https://legacy.reactjs.org/blog/2018/03/27/update-on-async-rendering.html#initializing-state](https://legacy.reactjs.org/blog/2018/03/27/update-on-async-rendering.html#initializing-state)
 - [https://legacy.reactjs.org/blog/2018/03/27/update-on-async-rendering.html#gradual-migration-path](https://legacy.reactjs.org/blog/2018/03/27/update-on-async-rendering.html#gradual-migration-path)

### ~~componentWillReceiveProps()?~~

```ts
optional componentWillReceiveProps(nextProps: Readonly<P>, nextContext: any): void
```

Called when the component may be receiving new props.
React may call this even if props have not changed, so be sure to compare new and existing
props if you only want to handle changes.

Calling [Component.setState](/api/react/Class.Component.mdx#setstate) generally does not trigger this method.

Note: the presence of NewLifecycle.getSnapshotBeforeUpdate getSnapshotBeforeUpdate
or StaticLifecycle.getDerivedStateFromProps getDerivedStateFromProps prevents
this from being invoked.

#### еҸӮж•°

| иҢғеӣҙ | зұ»еһӢ |
| ------ | ------ |
| `nextProps` | `Readonly`\<`P`\> |
| `nextContext` | `any` |

#### иҝ”еӣһ

`void`

#### е·Іиў«ејғз”Ё

16.3, use static StaticLifecycle.getDerivedStateFromProps getDerivedStateFromProps instead; will stop working in React 17

#### еҸӮйҳ…

 - [https://legacy.reactjs.org/blog/2018/03/27/update-on-async-rendering.html#updating-state-based-on-props](https://legacy.reactjs.org/blog/2018/03/27/update-on-async-rendering.html#updating-state-based-on-props)
 - [https://legacy.reactjs.org/blog/2018/03/27/update-on-async-rendering.html#gradual-migration-path](https://legacy.reactjs.org/blog/2018/03/27/update-on-async-rendering.html#gradual-migration-path)

### componentWillUnmount()?

```ts
optional componentWillUnmount(): void
```

Called immediately before a component is destroyed. Perform any necessary cleanup in this method, such as
cancelled network requests, or cleaning up any DOM elements created in `componentDidMount`.

#### иҝ”еӣһ

`void`

#### з»§жүҝиҮӘ

### ~~componentWillUpdate()?~~

```ts
optional componentWillUpdate(
   nextProps: Readonly<P>, 
   nextState: Readonly<S>, 
   nextContext: any): void
```

Called immediately before rendering when new props or state is received. Not called for the initial render.

Note: You cannot call [Component.setState](/api/react/Class.Component.mdx#setstate) here.

Note: the presence of NewLifecycle.getSnapshotBeforeUpdate getSnapshotBeforeUpdate
or StaticLifecycle.getDerivedStateFromProps getDerivedStateFromProps prevents
this from being invoked.

#### е·Іиў«ејғз”Ё

### getSnapshotBeforeUpdate()?

```ts
optional getSnapshotBeforeUpdate(prevProps: Readonly<P>, prevState: Readonly<S>): null | SS
```

Runs before React applies the result of [render](/api/react/Class.Component.mdx#render) to the document, and
returns an object to be given to [componentDidUpdate](/api/react/Class.Component.mdx#componentdidupdate). Useful for saving
things such as scroll position before [render](/api/react/Class.Component.mdx#render) causes changes to it.

Note: the presence of this method prevents any of the deprecated
lifecycle events from running.

#### еҸӮж•°

| иҢғеӣҙ | зұ»еһӢ |
| ------ | ------ |
| `prevProps` | `Readonly`\<`P`\> |
| `prevState` | `Readonly`\<`S`\> |

#### иҝ”еӣһ

`null` \| `SS`

#### з»§жүҝиҮӘ

`ComponentLifecycle.getSnapshotBeforeUpdate`

#### е®ҡд№үдәҺ

.pnpm/@types+react@18.3.28/node\_modules/@types/react/ts5.0/index.d.ts:1435

### shouldComponentUpdate()?

```ts
optional shouldComponentUpdate(
   nextProps: Readonly<P>, 
   nextState: Readonly<S>, 
   nextContext: any): boolean
```

Called to determine whether the change in props and state should trigger a re-render.

`Component` always returns true.
`PureComponent` implements a shallow comparison on props and state and returns true if any
props or states have changed.

If false is returned, [Component.render](/api/react/Class.Component.mdx#render), `componentWillUpdate`
and `componentDidUpdate` will not be called.

#### еҸӮж•°

| иҢғеӣҙ | зұ»еһӢ |
| ------ | ------ |
| `nextProps` | `Readonly`\<`P`\> |
| `nextState` | `Readonly`\<`S`\> |
| `nextContext` | `any` |

#### иҝ”еӣһ

`boolean`

#### з»§жүҝиҮӘ

`ComponentLifecycle.shouldComponentUpdate`

#### е®ҡд№үдәҺ

.pnpm/@types+react@18.3.28/node\_modules/@types/react/ts5.0/index.d.ts:1389
